Issue No.ll • 	June 2012
How To (Legally) 	Get Other People To
Write Your En1ails For You ... Without
Then1 Even Knowing About It
Hey Email Playa,
Today I'm going to start this issue off by revealing one of the coolest ways to write
emails you'll likely ever see. It's a very simple technique that has allowed me to write
emails sometimes in as little as 4 minutes (or less) and always very quickly. Plus, with this
technique, you will never have to wonder if your subject lines are any "good" or if the
topic about which you write is interesting to your market. In fact, there's a very good
chance the topic you write about, the theme you put forth and the subject line you use
will be such a good match for your readers, it will literally astonish you both (you and your
readers).
And the best part?
If there's a "lazy way" to write emails... this is it!
I'm not Mr. Lazy when it comes to this stuff. But nor will I turn down a legitimately
good way to create emails quickly and with quality. So without further ado, let me
'splaineth this "technique" I'm talking about so you can start using it. Here. we. GO...

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
1. Find popular forum.s in your ~narket, where your custo~ners like to
gather. This is mandatory research anyway if you're going to sell anything online,
as far as I'm concerned. In fact, when I used to do client work (or on the rare
occasions when I still do) and I don't really know the market, I send the client a
detailed list of questions. One of which is "what forums and blogs do your market
hang out at?" It's some of the best market intel you can get.
2. Look for the MOST viewed posts. Most forum software platforms these
days make this super easy, where you click on the "views" heading and it will sort
the posts/threads in order of highest number of views.
3. See those m.ost viewed forum. post subjects? Something about them is
getting people fired up. Those forum post titles arc resonating with your market.
Even if they seem tame or kind of weak, there is SOMETHING about them that's
getting attention. You can often take them almost word-for-word and use them as
email subject lines! (Personally, I change them up a little, you don't want to violate
any copyright laws -- what's important is the IDEAS in the post headlines).
Example: One of my best selling weight loss emails was written this way. I had no
idea the market was so concerned about having their heavy pies tagged on
FaceBook -- when I used that theme in an email it kicked gluteus assimus. All
because of this technique I'm showing you.
And make no mistake about it:
This goes WAY beyond just subject lines.
I have used this secret to write tons of emails in multiple niches. In fact, when I first
got into the prostate problem niche I would scour forums and just look at what people
were complaining about. What symptoms they were suffering through. What solutions
they were using. This in tel gave me a valuable leg up in the markets I've written to. I
could use the ~narket's exact language they use to describe the problem (this
is HUGE if you want ads and emails that dominate). I would alter my products by adding
solutions to the problems I didn't even know existed! And most important of all ... because
I'm speaking my market's language... because I'm talking WITH them (and not just TO
them) ... they took my content far more seriously, were more likely to consume my
products (mucho important) and solve their problem (and thus, were more likely to be

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
repeat buyers). There are soooooo many benefits to doing this, and no drawbacks. And
again, when you have your subject lines and themes practically handed to you on a silver
platter, it makes writing your emails ~ fast. I'm not saying to copy and paste what
others have written. Don't do that. But you CAN use the words, their stories ("I know of a
guy who once suffered from ... " and then just repeat someone's story in your own words),
and the ideas in these forums. And by the way, just because I say forum, doesn't mean you
can't do this with blog comments, too. Blogs about your topic are GOLD MINES of info
like this. People tend to banter and write off the cuff when commenting on blog posts,
and you'll get some of the best material to work with. Or, at the very least, you'll see what
questions your market is asking and what hot buttons they have.
And hey, this isn't just for weight loss or prostate problems or whatever, either.
I have yet to see a market you can't do this with.
A friend of mine used it the way I just explained to you to sell his product to
Internet marketers. Here's what he said:
so bro,
you know how i 	told you i 	got clickbank leads and they werent
converting?
i 	used your forum research method along with a template and been
producing emails specifically relating to clickbank and it's been
producing 1-2 sales a day.
you've made me a lot of money, 	i 	feel bad that you're not getting a cut
haha :P but not TOO bad lol
my Clickbank leads are getting as many as 2 emails a day and they're
warming up from COLD traffic pretty quick as a result.
that's all ••• just wanted to share my excitement :D
I'm telling you, 	this works!
(Note: 1-2 sales per day n1ay not sound like a lot,
but his list was sn1all, his product was higher ticket,
and before doing this he'd only gotten n1aybe 1-2 sales per week ... )

Do it!

What Never To Write
In An En1ail Subject Line
Speaking of lazy email tips ...
Another way to cheat your way to email success (well, hey, it IS cheating, but in a
gQ.Q.d way... ) is to read old Mel Martin ads. Who was Mel Martin, you ask? Mel was
perhaps one of the top 2 or 3 greatest copywriters of the 20th century. He was so good ...
that Marty Edelston (founder of the $100 million per year Boardroom, Inc.) had him on
secret retainer. In other words, people would ask who his copywriter was, and who was
writing all these amazingly profitable ads and he had a deal with Mel that neither of them
would reveal who Mel was. Every single direct mail copywriter today who works for the
"big boys" (that I'm aware of, at least) reveres Mel Martin. He was especially famous for
his teaser bullets (he was the guy who originally coined the term "fascinations") which are
now a staple of direct mail ads by many of the biggest companies out there. Mel was, for
example, the guy who wrote the famous "What never to eat on an airplane" headline.
There's an old video teaching by the great Eugene Schwartz where he talked about Mel
Martin's headlines a bit. And he admitted that most people could never figure out how
they pulled so well because they weren't screaming big claims or benefits. Gene's theory?
There's an "implied mechanism" in his headlines that makes people want to know the
answer to whatever question the bullet/headlines were posing.
OK, so what's this got to do with your emails???
-4-

-- 4 of 16 --

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
Everything!
Because Mel Martin headlines and bullet points are PERFECT templates for email
subject lines. "What never to eat on an airplane if you're overweight" was one I've used
quite successfully. Or "What never to test in your sales letters". See? No big claim. No big
benefit. No "big" anything whatsoever. They are just statements that create an itch ... a
powerful, annoying itch ... that MUST be scratched if you custom-fit it to your market.
But, you ask, where can you find Mel Martin bullets and headlines? Where can you go?
Why. .. you don't have to go anywhere -- because I've listed a bunch of them FOR you
below for your swiping pleasure:
• 	What never to eat on an airplane
• 	What airlines won't tell you
• 	What car dealers charge you for twice
• 	How to get a store's best price
• 	When travel brochures are BALONEY
• 	Diets that make you heavier
• 	How to get your doctor on the phone
• 	How auto repair shops pad your bill
• 	Best credit card to use abroad
• 	Condominium buyers alert
• 	Secret word makes ambulance come faster
• 	Make your car "invisible" to highway radar
• 	How to foil a rapist

The above bullets/headlines are ALL easily adapted for email for virtually any kind
of market or product if you use just a bit of creativity. Gust do the ol' switcheroo -- switch
out the details in the bullet with your market/product details). Notice the implied
mechanism Gene Schwartz talked about in each one? How they create an irresistible itch
of curiosity that MUST be scratched? (And remember a few issues ago when we covered
the all-mighty power of curiosity in emails...) Anyway, use 'em and profit from 'em. It just
don't get much easier and better than this when it comes to writing email subject lines ...
Hard Core PWN
Your Competition
As I promised before, we're going to revisit the methodology of learning how to
"soft" teach vs "hard" teach in your emails. People who learn the art and science of soft
teaching (and it does take a bit of practice...) tend to do much better than those who hard
teach in emails. Hard teaching is when you give hard "how to" instruction in an email
(that's in your paid product). Soft teaching being very informational, but not instructional.
Anyway, we talked about this a few months back and I want to revisit it again today with
a few more examples of how to do soft teaching to increase your sales. Mter this, you will

have a nice leg up over your competition who are probably spending hours per day trying
to "out-content" everyone by moving free lines or whatever.
Anyway, one great way to do soft teaching is to bring awareness of a problem your
market has but probably isn't aware exists. These kinds of emails ARE valuable. They do
teach vital information your market needs (and will be glad to know about). But they
aren't giving hard instruction. And the trick is to make your product the "hero" that saves
the day from this secret foe your market may not even have realized existed a few minutes
ago. Here's an example:
Subject: Merchant account kicks my app
Ben,
Since it's Halloween time, 	how 	'bout a horror story?
Recently, 	I was talking to Doberman can about merchant accounts.
Dan has been selling health supplements for over a decade, 	has an
impeccable "money trail" 	record to look at as being ethical and
transparent. 	And basically runs a business as honest as the day is
long.
But, 	that didn't stop 	'em from shutting him downl
Talk about a horror story •••
Imagine having your business running like normal, 	then one day
waking up to dozens 	(maybe hundreds) of "declined" orders.
With no warning.
And no 	"heads up" whatsoever.
Someone at the bank decides your business is suddenly "high risk"
(even though you have zero charge backs or complaints on file) 	and
your business is effectively shut down ••• 	in the blink of a
bureaucrat's eye.
Yikes.
That's scary.
(Don't worry about Dan though, 	he's a smart cookie, 	and in the
next "Email Players" 	issue -- http://www.EmailPlayers.com -- I'm

Anyway, where was I?
Oh, 	yeah, 	now, 	you may be thinking:
"But Ben! 	I 	don't sell supplements. 	I 	sell an eBook -- information
--with nothing controversial about it ••• "
You think that makes you safe?
Forget regular merchant accounts for a second.
Even 3rd party payment processors that have been selling info
products for years are following suit.
Take yours truly, 	for example.
I'm gearing up to promote a high ticket product with affiliates.
It's a physical product.
And I 	don't want to mess with paying affiliates myself.
So I 	thought I'd use 2Checkout.
They been around a while and are reputable.
Plus, 	I 	have even been an affiliate for info publishers who have
used them to sell products very similar-in content and price as my
product.
But Saturday, 	I 	get an email that says:
(And I 	quote ••• )
"Unfortunately, we can no longer support sales from any website
that would be classified as a direct marketing website by Visa and
MasterCard regulations. 	This is a policy that has been passed down
to us from our merchant provider."
Application denied!
(Notice, 	their merchant account made the decision, 	not them.)

"Email's coming back
in a big i&$*1 	way"
Oh yeah, 	baby!
And it's about time, 	too.
Every week I'm seeing even the most hardcore, 	"fire breathing" Web
2.0 purists starting to come around to the fact that relying on RSS
feeds, 	FaceBook, 	Twitter, 	YouTube, 	etc to sell is not only costing
them a fat chunk of dineros, 	but is not nearly as fun, 	either.
It's kind of funny sometimes.
It's like email is a shiny new object again.
Something exciting and unique.
But, 	not everyone's convinced.
There are still plenty of naysayers nattering on about email fears
(real and imagined) 	like a bureaucrat some day deciding to tax it
(so it's not free anymore) ••• 	or that it'll fall off the face of
the earth and be overtaken by FaceBook messages, 	tweets or whatever
new gimmick hits the corner next.
I wouldn't worry about that happening.
But, what if it did?
What if email was outlawed or made obsolete?
What of it?
The communication skillz you learn by writing and selling your
product with regular emails can be used for ANYTHING else that
comes down the pike.
And I 	do mean anything.
Think of it this way:
The same principles that make profitable email work, 	also work for
articles, 	podcasts, 	videos, 	social media, 	blog posts, 	and even
sales letters or public speaking.

This entire email was designed to glorifY the benefits of email, address certain
objections my market has, and inspire them to want to learn email and be great at it. You
can do this with whatever YOU sell, too. Just do the same thing as in the above email.
Even if you can't use it as an exact template, you can write about what you sell in a way
that makes using it seem fun and exciting. I think this is such a powerful way to do email
that I'll be writing about it again (with more examples) in future issues. In the meantime,
one of the tried-and-true soft teaching methods: Giving them a list of things they're doing
WRONG. I learned this from Matt Furey (I learned a lot about email from studying his
emails and courses) and it's extremely powerful. Pretty simple, too. Just list ofT common
mistakes your market is making (or that you used to make). Again, it's not hard instruction
--but it IS informational and valuable. And if you do it right it will increase the chances
of your readers wanting to buy from you:
Subject Line: 	10 things that kept me broke as a 	joke
Ben,
Let's change things up a bit today.
Let's rap about finances.

However, 	before going any further, 	a disclaimer:
Here at Casa de Settle we're FAR from being "rich" and nowhere NEAR
our financial goals. 	And so the only reason I 	feel even somewhat
qualified to discuss this at all is because I 	have
made so MANY dumb financial mistakes, 	I 	could fill an entire 500
page book.
Below are 10 	"doozy" mistakes I made.
If you're doing them, 	then as the proverbial Monopoly game says •••
STOP.
Do not pass "go."
And do not collect $200 	(you'll probably just blow it).
Frankly, 	just by NOT doing these 10 things, 	you can often reverse
even the most dire of financial situations over time.
At least, 	that was the case for us.
Ready?
OK then, 	here. 	we. 	go •••
1. 	Purposely staying in debt
2. 	Blaming others for your problems
3. 	Falling for hype
4. 	Drinking biz opp "kool-aid"
5. 	Giving up too soon 	(most people are 	just INCHES away from
success, 	and don't even realize it)
6. 	Clinging to bad ideas
7. 	Not being open to new opportunities
8. 	Listening to naysayers
9. 	Letting your emotions control you
-13-

-- 13 of 16 --

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
10. Carrying buckets instead of building "pipelines."
OK, 	I 	think the first 9 are self explanatory, 	but number 10
probably needs a bit of 	•splaining.
Especially if you haven't read much Robert Kiyosaki.
Basically, 	it breaks down like this:
Imagine you're on an island village and have been hired to bring
that village water. 	Most people would go to the lake, 	fill as many
buckets as they can carry, 	and bring them back. 	But not so the
savvy person. 	Instead of laboriously filling and carrying water
buckets, 	he'd instead take the time and energy to build a pipeline
to bring the water to the village -- greatly freeing up his time,
energy and resources.
This is a BIG game-changer when applied to finances.
And is definitely worth some serious study.
(Just Google "Kiyosaki pipeline")
Anyway, 	again, 	I'm no financial guru (or even a goo-rool)
But I've made a TON of mistakes.
Mistakes you can now easily avoid.
Ben Settle
P.S. 	Another big financial mistake 	(for entrepreneurs) 	is not
focusing on using email more aggressively. 	And it cost me
a LOT of sales over the years. 	You can easily avoid
this hum-dinger by going here:
http://www.EmailPlayers.com

someone had given me ... and that's a great way to approach this: List mistakes you used
to make). And, hopefully, it inspires them to want to buy your product, too.

THE 	EMAIL PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
How To ''Re-anbnate''
Dead Buyers
Let's "squeeze" one more lesson in before wrapping up. One question I've been
getting quite a bit lately is from people who are new to my ways of doing email, have a
list, but they're lists are essentially "dead." In other words, they haven't mailed that list in
months (or years!) and they are not sure how to reactivate those lists in a way that won't
make people unsubscribe in droves or whatever.
It's a good question.
And, if this is you, here's all you do:
Send them an update to the product they bought (if it's a buyers list) or an update
to the free opt-in premium (you did give them a premium for opting in, right. .. ?) if it's a
prospect list. It can even be as simple as this:
Subject Line: ___ Update
Name,
Long time no hear 	:)
Back on ___ (insert date, 	most auto-responder companies have this
feature) 	you bought ___ product 	(or you 	joined my __ email tips
list). And I wanted you to know I 	have made an update to it.
You can download the update free at:
(LINK)
I'll send another email like this tomorrow just in case.
SIGNATURE
And then, of course, you send another email the next day (maybe even another
after that) with the download link. No, you won't reactivate everyone. But you have a nice
excuse to contact them and those who are still interested will likely download the update,
remember you, and be prepped to start receiving more tips from you in the future.
-15-
